item.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-13 - Today's Summary
item.description: The dominant pattern today is the sustained breakout of structured agent skill packs and memory frameworks, led by 'addyosmani/agent-skills' (57.8K stars, +2,565 in 24h) and 'Panniantong/Agent-Reach' (27.1K stars, +704 in 24h). These projects encode senior engineering workflows and give agents zero-friction access to external platforms, signaling a shift from generic agent frameworks to production-ready, skill-specific tooling. Meanwhile, 'eyaltoledano/claude-task-master' (27.5K stars) shows a sudden breakout with 9.1x acceleration, integrating task management directly into AI coding environments via MCP. The AI Agent Framework category leads with the highest average daily delta (591), while Coding Agent and MCP categories also show strong sustained attention. The quietness of RAG and knowledge base categories (only 1 repo, low delta) suggests developer focus is moving from retrieval to agent execution and memory persistence. If this trend holds, we may see further specialization in agent skill libraries and memory backends as the next layer of infrastructure. Top projects: eyaltoledano/claude-task-master, stas00/ml-engineering, topoteretes/cognee, addyosmani/agent-skills, Panniantong/Agent-Reach, esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix, nexu-io/open-design, rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch, sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills, getagentseal/codeburn, kenn-io/agentsview, HKUDS/nanobot Note: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。 Note: daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。 Note: Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。 Note: Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

item.title: [sudden_breakout] eyaltoledano/claude-task-master (+73 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: An AI-powered task-management system that integrates directly into Cursor, Lovable, Windsurf, and other AI coding environments via MCP. Why it matters: With 27,315 stars and a 7-day average delta of 7.1 stars/day, the project shows sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio of 2.8 indicates a sudden breakout — interest is intensifying rapidly. The 24-hour delta of 11 stars confirms continued momentum. Developer insight: The rise of MCP-based tools like Taskmaster signals a shift from ad-hoc AI chat to structured, persistent workflows. Developers who adopt these patterns early may gain a significant productivity edge. Risk: Medium

item.title: [sudden_breakout] stas00/ml-engineering (+18 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: RAG / Knowledge Base Description: A comprehensive open book of methodologies, tools, and commands for training and fine-tuning large language and multi-modal models. Why it matters: With 18,015 stars and a sudden acceleration ratio of 2.9 (high), the repo is experiencing a breakout. The 24-hour star delta of 20 and 7-day average of 6 indicate a sharp increase in attention, likely driven by the growing need for hands-on ML engineering guidance as LLM training becomes more accessible. Developer insight: Signals a shift toward open, community-driven knowledge sharing in ML engineering, reducing the barrier for smaller teams to adopt large-scale training practices. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] topoteretes/cognee (+36 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: Cognee is an open-source AI memory platform that gives agents persistent long-term memory via a self-hosted knowledge graph. Why it matters: Cognee is experiencing a sudden breakout: 17.8k stars, +36 stars in 24h, +116 in 7d, with a high acceleration of 2.1. The 24h delta (36) is more than double the 3-day average (17.3), indicating a sharp spike in attention. This is not just steady growth—something has triggered a surge in interest, making it a hot project right now. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward standardized, open-source memory infrastructure for agents. As agents become more autonomous, persistent memory is a must-have. Cognee's approach—combining graph and vector storage with ontology grounding—points to a future where memory is not just a cache but a structured, evolving knowledge base. Developers should watch for integration patterns with popular agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGPT, etc.) and consider contributing to the ecosystem. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] addyosmani/agent-skills (+2565 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A production-grade pack of 24 structured skills and slash commands that encode senior engineering workflows for AI coding agents. Why it matters: The repo has exploded from 0 to 51,894 stars with a 24h delta of 2,051 and a 7d delta of 3,844. The acceleration ratio is 4 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral spike driven by the release of a comprehensive, well-documented skill pack for multiple agent platforms. Developer insight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc agent prompting to structured, skill-based agent programming. It suggests that the future of AI-assisted development lies in reusable, verifiable skill packs rather than raw LLM prompts. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such skill ecosystems. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] Panniantong/Agent-Reach (+704 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI coding agents zero-friction access to read and search across Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more — without any API fees. Why it matters: With 26,660 stars and a 7-day average of 800 new stars per day, Agent Reach is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The 24-hour delta of 597 and acceleration of 0.7 indicate a steady, organic surge — not a one-day spike. This momentum suggests broad, ongoing adoption among AI developers who need their agents to interact with the open web. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward 'agent infrastructure' as a new category: tools that abstract away platform-specific barriers (API costs, login requirements, anti-bot measures) so agents can operate freely. The sustained star growth indicates strong product-market fit and a clear demand for open-source, community-maintained alternatives to paid data APIs. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix (+386 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability. Why it matters: The repo is gaining 455 stars/day on average over 7 days, with a 24h delta of 386 and a high acceleration of 0.8. With 21,715 total stars and a sustained hot trend, it's clearly resonating with developers seeking efficient, DeepSeek-optimized coding agents. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward model-specific, performance-optimized coding agents that minimize API costs through caching. Developers should consider how prefix caching can be leveraged in their own tools. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] nexu-io/open-design (+609 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your laptop into an agent-native design studio, generating prototypes, decks, images, and videos via 21+ coding agent CLIs. Why it matters: With 64,221 stars, a 24-hour delta of +609, and a 7-day average of +663 stars/day, Open Design is sustaining explosive growth. The acceleration ratio of 1 indicates steady, high-velocity adoption rather than a sudden spike. The recent 0.10.0 release (all-in-one agentic workspace) and the launch of the AMR model router likely fuel continued interest. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward agent-native design tools where the CLI and MCP become the primary interface. For AI developers, Open Design shows how to build composable, local-first systems that leverage multiple coding agents and model providers, reducing dependency on any single vendor. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch (+394 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact. Why it matters: With 31,756 stars, a 24-hour delta of +319, and a 7-day average of +421.6, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.9 (high) indicates strong organic momentum without a sudden spike, suggesting broad, steady adoption. The project's claim of 150,639 readers and 241,669 page views in the last 30 days further confirms real-world traction. Developer insight: The project signals a shift in how AI education is consumed: developers want hands-on, code-first, artifact-producing curricula that integrate with their existing tools (Claude, Cursor, etc.). The emphasis on MCP servers and agent skills points to a growing ecosystem around agentic workflows. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills (+178 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: Installable library of 1,541+ agentic skills for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and other AI coding assistants. Why it matters: With 40,559 stars, 6,545 forks, and a sustained 7-day average of 94.4 stars/day (24h delta: 150, acceleration: 1.5), this project shows strong, consistent growth. The recent release v12.4.0 and active commits within 7 days signal ongoing maintenance and feature expansion, making it a reliable choice for developers seeking structured agent skill libraries. Developer insight: The project signals a shift toward standardized, reusable agent instructions, similar to package managers for code. It may become a de facto standard for skill distribution, reducing fragmentation in the AI coding tool ecosystem. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] getagentseal/codeburn (+65 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: CodeBurn is a local TUI dashboard that tracks token usage and cost across 25+ AI coding tools, helping developers see exactly where their AI budget goes. Why it matters: With 7,939 stars, a 7-day delta of +258 stars, and a sustained acceleration of 1.4, CodeBurn is experiencing strong organic growth. The 24h delta of +53 stars and three-day average of 38.3 indicate consistent high interest. This is not a sudden spike but a steady climb, suggesting the project has found product-market fit among AI developers. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward cost-aware AI development. As AI coding becomes a line item in engineering budgets, tools like CodeBurn will become essential. Developers who ignore cost observability risk overspending; those who adopt it gain a competitive edge in efficiency. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] kenn-io/agentsview (+417 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: Local-first session intelligence and analytics for coding agents, supporting Claude Code, Codex, and more than 20 other agents. Also: 100x faster replacement for ccusage! Why it matters: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +417，总 stars 2,196，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。 Developer insight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。 Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] HKUDS/nanobot (+70 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: Lightweight, open-source AI agent for your tools, chats, and workflows. Why it matters: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +70，总 stars 44,152，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。 Developer insight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。 Risk: Low
